sections.paragraph: m. ashes. (Perhaps this is a primitive, kindred to the word עָפָר, and pr. denotes dust and earth, compare cinis, from the Gr. κόνις, and the etymology of the pr.n. אֶפְרַיִם, אֶפְרָת. Similar is the Gr. τέφρα. [or from אָפַר II.]). Num 19:9, 10 Num 19:10 2Sa 13:19. It is used principally in speaking of mourning, Jer 6:26 Lam 3:16 to which belong the phrases, Psal. 102:10 , “I have eaten ashes like bread.” Est 4:1, וַיִלְבַּשׁ שַׂק וָאֵפֶר “and he put on sackcloth and ashes,” compare Est 4:3 Isa 58:5. Paronomastically put together, עָפָר וָאֵפֶר “dust and ashes,” Job 30:19 42:6 . Metaph. used of anything light and fallacious. Job 13:12, מִשְׁלֵי אֵפֶר “maxims of ashes,” i.e. vain and fallacious. Isa 44:20, רֹועֶה אֵפֶר “he follows after ashes,” sc. as driven by the wind; i.q. elsewhere, “to follow after the wind,” compare רָעָה. As to its difference from דֶּשֶׁן, see below at that word.
